regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean ports including San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos will experience unsettled weather today with easterly winds 10-16 knots and frequent rain showers, especially near San Juan where rain chances reach 90 percent. Mariners should prepare for reduced visibility and choppy seas; secure loose gear and exercise caution when navigating or anchoring in exposed areas such as St. Thomas and St. Maarten harbors. Fishing captains should expect intermittent rain and moderate winds, planning trips during brief lulls to avoid rough conditions. Conditions will improve gradually over the next two days with decreasing rain chances and stable easterly winds, allowing for safer passage and more comfortable passenger activity.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean conditions for March 7, 2026 show scattered rain showers near Cozumel with temperatures around 78 degrees and east-southeast winds steady at 11 knots. Expect similar conditions today with moderate winds and a 28 percent chance of brief showers, easing to partly cloudy skies and lower rain chances by day three. Mariners should exercise caution entering exposed anchorages in Roatan and Belize City during showers, as gusty winds may develop. Passengers should keep rain gear handy but anticipate improving conditions for outdoor activities by midweek.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region conditions today feature clear skies and light east-northeast winds at 6 knots, with temperatures near 71 degrees Fahrenheit in Nassau. Expect gentle east winds increasing to 12 knots by tomorrow, bringing partly cloudy skies with minimal rain risk across Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos. By day three, winds pick up to 16 knots with overcast skies and light drizzle, which may reduce visibility and affect small craft operations. Mariners should secure light vessels in well-protected anchorages like Nassau Harbor and monitor for increasing east winds when planning offshore fishing or tender runs. Passengers should enjoy calm seas but prepare for cooler, damp conditions midweek.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean conditions for March 7, 2026 show steady east winds around 17 knots with temperatures near 78 degrees and partly cloudy skies at Aruba. Expect winds to increase to 19-21 knots over the next three days with a slight chance of rain showers, mainly on day one. Mariners should ensure rigging and anchoring gear are secured, especially in exposed anchorages like Cartagena where easterly winds can produce moderate chop. Cruise passengers and fishing operators in Curacao and Bonaire should plan for stable but breezy conditions with low rain risk, ideal for outdoor activities but stay alert for brief showers near Aruba.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean weather for March 7, 2026 shows scattered rain showers across ports including Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Cozumel, and Costa Maya. Grand Cayman reports 79 degrees with ENE winds at 14 knots and light showers, continuing through the next three days with winds steady between 12 and 14 knots. Mariners should maintain a sharp lookout for brief rain squalls that could reduce visibility and cause localized choppy seas, especially near coastal anchorages like Belize City and Roatan. Cruise and fishing captains are advised to secure loose gear and plan for intermittent wet conditions while keeping navigation aids and radar systems active.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles weather today features moderate ENE winds around 17 knots with temperatures near 78 degrees Fahrenheit and partly cloudy skies. Expect isolated showers around Barbados with a 43 percent chance of rain; conditions will be similar across neighboring islands including St. Lucia and Martinique. Rain showers will increase tonight into Day 1 with winds strengthening to 20 knots, so vessels in Antigua and St. Kitts should secure loose gear and monitor local forecasts. Fishing captains should plan for intermittent rain and moderate seas, while passengers should carry light rain gear when ashore, especially around Barbados and St. Vincent.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles region weather on March 7, 2026, is currently clear with temperatures near 56 degrees Fahrenheit and light NNW winds at 6 knots. Expect steady warming today with NE winds increasing to 19 knots and clear skies; ideal conditions for marina operations at Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach. By day two, winds shift to ESE at 17 knots with overcast skies and minimal rain risk, warranting attention to Catalina Island anchorage where afternoon sea breezes may cause some chop. Day three brings cooler temperatures near 67 degrees with SSE winds at 14 knots, maintaining overcast but stable conditions—fishing captains should plan for moderate seas and monitor wind shifts near Marina del Rey.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region weather for March 7, 2026: Currently 56F with light east winds at 5 knots and clear skies; no precipitation expected today. Winds increase to 16 knots from the ENE with overcast skies by afternoon, though rain chances remain minimal at 3 percent. Expect a shift to southwest winds around 10 knots with drizzle and a 33 percent chance of light rain on day two, continuing into day three with temperatures dropping to the upper 60s. Mariners should secure loose gear and monitor conditions at Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos, as increasing winds and drizzle may reduce visibility and affect anchorage comfort; fishing vessels should plan for lighter activity during the drizzle periods.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas region will see stable, overcast conditions through March 7 with light to moderate northwest winds at 13 knots and temperatures in the low 70s Fahrenheit. Expect similar weather at San Jose del Cabo and La Paz, with minimal rain chances under 5 percent over the next three days. Mariners should prepare for steady winds favorable for anchoring in protected bays but maintain vigilance for gusts near 15 knots. Cruise passengers and fishing captains can plan outdoor activities with low risk of weather disruptions, but keep a light jacket handy due to overcast skies and coastal breezes.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region reports mild temperatures near 76 to 77 degrees with light southerly winds shifting west-northwest over the next three days. Expect intermittent rain showers today and tomorrow with a 25% chance, easing to mainly overcast skies and minimal precipitation by day three. South-southwest winds at 9 knots today may cause slight chop in Mazatlan and Topolobampo harbors; caution advised when maneuvering small craft. Fishing captains should plan for variable conditions and monitor updated forecasts before heading offshore, while cruise passengers should keep rain gear handy for daytime excursions.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta and surrounding ports including Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala will experience mild conditions today with temperatures near 78 degrees Fahrenheit and light northerly winds at 3 knots. Skies are partly cloudy with no significant precipitation expected, making it ideal for daytime operations and passenger excursions. Over the next three days, expect steady overcast conditions with temperatures rising slightly to around 81 degrees and moderate northwest winds at 8 to 9 knots. Mariners should monitor wind shifts for anchoring in Yelapa and Chacala where holding can be affected by changing wind directions; fishing operations will benefit from calm seas but maintain vigilance for any late afternoon cloud build-up.
